title: Match queries: search by team, date, competition, season
evidence: tools.go:23-49 search_matches supports team1, team2, season, competition params; TestSearchMatches_TwoTeams, TestSearchMatches_ByCompetition, TestSearchMatches_CopaDoBrasil, TestSearchMatches_Libertadores all pass
suggestion: Date range filtering: description mentions 'date range' but parameters missing (no date_start/date_end)

title: Date range filtering for match queries not implemented
evidence: tools.go:23-49 search_matches tool schema lacks date_start/date_end parameters; description promises 'date range' support but parameters missing from schema
suggestion: Add date_start and date_end parameters to search_matches and implement date-based filtering

title: Team statistics: wins/losses/draws, goals, performance metrics
evidence: tools.go:51-79 get_team_stats returns W/L/D/GF/GA/WR; TestGetTeamStats_Basic, TestGetTeamStats_HomeRecord pass; supports season/competition/home_only/away_only filters

title: Player search: by name, nationality, club, position, rating
evidence: tools.go:81+ search_players with name, nationality, club, position, min_overall params; TestSearchPlayers_ByNationality, TestSearchPlayers_ByClub, TestSearchPlayers_ByName, TestSearchPlayers_MinOverall all pass
suggestion: All filter dimensions implemented and tested

title: Competition standings calculated from match results
evidence: tools.go get_standings; TestGetStandings_2019, TestGetStandings_MissingSeasonReturnsError pass; calculates league points from match outcomes

title: Head-to-head team comparisons with win/draw/loss records
evidence: tools.go get_head_to_head; TestGetHeadToHead_FlamengoCorinthians, TestGetHeadToHead_MissingTeamReturnsError pass; returns H2H stats for each team

title: Statistical aggregates: biggest wins, home vs away records
evidence: tools.go get_biggest_wins; TestGetBiggestWins_TopTen, TestGetBiggestWins_ByCompetition pass; supports filtering by competition

title: All 6 CSV datasets loadable and queryable
evidence: data.go LoadAll() loads Brasileirão, Copa do Brasil, Libertadores, BR-Football-Dataset, novo_campeonato_brasileiro, fifa_data; TestDataLoading_AllCSVsLoad loads 23954 matches + 18207 players
suggestion: All datasets verified present and parseable

title: MCP protocol server (JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio with Content-Length framing)
evidence: server.go MCPServer implements initialize, tools/list, tools/call; readMessage/writeMessage handle LSP-style framing; TestDispatchTool_ValidTools pass
suggestion: Protocol fully functional for LLM integration

title: Team name normalization: handle state suffixes, accents, variations
evidence: normalize.go normalizeTeamName() removes state suffixes (-SP, -RJ, -MG, etc.); TestNormalizeTeamName covers 7 test cases (Palmeiras-SP→Palmeiras, etc.)

title: Date/time parsing: ISO, Brazilian DD/MM/YYYY, with timestamps
evidence: data.go parseDate() handles multiple formats; TestParseDate passes; test data includes timestamps like '2012-05-19 18:30:00'

title: UTF-8 encoding for Portuguese text (accents, cedilla, special chars)
evidence: Go strings handle UTF-8 natively; data files load with proper diacritics (Grêmio, São Paulo, Fortaleza, Atlético-MG visible in test data)

title: TestSearchMatches_TeamAndSeason skipped: no Palmeiras 2023 data
evidence: mcp_test.go:210 t.Skip("No Palmeiras matches in 2023 - dataset may not include this year")
suggestion: Dataset contains matches up to ~2019; 2023 season data not in provided CSV files

title: Clean modular architecture: entry point, protocol, 6 tools, data layer
evidence: main.go (entry), server.go (MCP protocol), tools.go (query tools), data.go (CSV loading), normalize.go (data normalization), mcp_test.go (28 passing tests)
suggestion: Clear separation of concerns enables maintainability

title: Excellent performance: full test suite 11s, individual tests <1s each
evidence: go test ./... completes in 11.166s; individual tests 0.24-1.06s; meets <2s SLA for simple queries
suggestion: Performance scales well for knowledge graph operations

title: Zero external dependencies: pure Go stdlib only
evidence: go.sum has 0 entries; only stdlib imports (encoding/json, bufio, io, log, os, sort, strings)
suggestion: Minimal supply chain risk, maximum portability
